final_answer: COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by a coronavirus called SARS-CoV-2. The term 'COVID-19' stands for 'corona', 'virus', and 'disease'. This disease is contagious and can lead to serious complications, including respiratory distress and blood clots. COVID-19 was declared a global health emergency by the World Health Organization in March 2020, following its spread worldwide starting in January 2020, which led to a pandemic in 2020.
